mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW i Series electric motor replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW i Series electric motor typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — with dual-motor models costing more. Battery replacement costs £5,000–£15,000+ depending on model and battery size. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW i Series model is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The i4 eDrive40 — proven Gen5 eDrive technology, single motor simplicity, and established battery technology. The iX xDrive40 is also excellent. Avoid the early i3 60 Ah (battery degradation) and the i8 (hybrid complexity).

mainEntity.name: Which BMW i Series model should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No model outright — but an early i3 60 Ah (2013–2016) without a battery health check is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The i8 is a fantastic car but carries hybrid complexity — budget for specialist servicing. A 42 kWh i3, i4, or iX has no such caveat.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my i Series motor or battery needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By issue: whining noise under load (motor bearing wear), reduced range (battery degradation), increased charging times (battery degradation), charging port failure (charging system), reduced power (i8 hybrid system). On the i3, any significant range reduction is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a service; ignored, it's a £3,000–£15,000+ replacement.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW i Series motor or battery?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends entirely on model and component. Against a £20,000–£30,000 i4, a £3,000–£5,000 motor replacement is 10–25% — ✅ safe. A £7,000–£11,000 battery replacement is 23–55% — ⚠️ watch. Against a £8,000–£15,000 early i3, a £5,000–£10,000 battery replacement is 33–125% — ❌ walk away. Our 60% BMW threshold is the line: below 60% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW i Series should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: i4 eDrive40 with Gen5 eDrive — 286 hp, 340 miles of range, proven technology, and sensible used values (£20,000–£30,000). For the budget option, i3 120 Ah (42 kWh) — the largest battery is the one to buy; budget for a battery health check before purchase. For the future, Neue Klasse i3 — 469 hp, 800V architecture, and near-M3 power. For the collector, i8 — a future classic with hybrid complexity.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW i Series battery capacity?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; the battery capacity is also visible in the iDrive system under charging settings. Or tell us your model year and variant (i3 60 Ah, i4 eDrive40, iX xDrive50) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW i4 vs Tesla Model 3 — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The i4 is significantly more premium and better built than the Model 3 — the i4's interior quality and build quality are superior. The Model 3 has better charging infrastructure access (Tesla Supercharger network) but the i4 is closing the gap. The i4's Gen5 eDrive is proven and reliable. Buy the i4 for premium quality; buy the Model 3 for charging network access.

hero.tagPill: BMW i Series • 2013–Present • 7 Models (i3, i4, i5, i7, i8, iX, iX3) • Electric Motors, PHEV, Neue Klasse 800V
hero.h1: i Series Electric Motors — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW i Series electric powertrain — from the original i3 to the Neue Klasse i3, the i4 Gran Coupé, i5, i7 luxury saloon, iX SUV, and the i8 plug-in hybrid supercar. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's electric revolution. Backed by real UK i Series enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

ownershipVerdict.h2: BMW i Series — The Ownership Verdict

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The i Series rule — battery is the new engine: an electric motor replacement at £2,000–£4,000 is routine; a battery pack replacement at £5,000–£15,000+ is the defining cost. Against a £15,000–£30,000 used i4/iX, battery replacement is 17–100% — ⚠️ watch on cheaper cars.
★★★★☆ — electric motors are inherently more reliable than combustion engines; battery degradation is the primary long-term concern; the i8's 1.5L three-cylinder engine adds complexity
i4 / iX with Gen5 eDrive — proven electric motors, established battery technology
i8 — hybrid complexity (electric motor + turbo 1.5L three-cylinder) and limited production (20,465 units)
i4 eDrive40 — 286 hp, 340-mile range, proven Gen5 eDrive, sensible used values
Neue Klasse i3 50 xDrive — 469 hp, 800V architecture, up to 900 km range, near-M3 power
Battery degradation / electric motor bearing wear [BMW-VERIFIED]
⚡ The Electric Revolution — BMW's vision for the future of mobility

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The i Series is BMW's electric future — from the pioneering i3 to the Neue Klasse revolution. Electric motors are simpler and more reliable than combustion engines, but batteries are the new engine: £5,000–£15,000+ to replace, and degradation is the defining long-term cost. Buy the newest battery technology you can afford.

atAGlance.h2: BMW i Series at a Glance

rows.value: Full Electric (BEV), Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV — i8 only)
Gen5 eDrive (400V) → Gen6 eDrive / Neue Klasse (800V)
i4 eDrive40 / iX xDrive40 — proven Gen5 eDrive

generations.h2: BMW i Series — Find Your Model
generations.subHeadline: Seven models, two decades of innovation, and one electric future. The right i Series depends on which model and which battery technology you choose.

cards.title: i3 — The Pioneer (2013–2022)
cards.verdict: BMW's first mass-produced electric car — a carbon-fibre city car with a design that still looks futuristic. The early 22 kWh models suffer from battery degradation; the 42 kWh (120 Ah) models are the ones to buy. The range-extender (REX) model adds complexity — buy pure EV.

cards.title: i8 — The Hybrid Supercar (2014–2020)
cards.meta: 2014–2020 • 1.5L turbo I3 + 131 hp electric motor • 369 hp combined • 20,465 produced
cards.verdict: The plug-in hybrid supercar that defined BMW's electric ambitions — butterfly doors, carbon-fibre chassis, and the B38 three-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor. The i8 is a future classic — but the hybrid complexity means specialist-only servicing. Buy the best-documented example you can afford.

cards.title: iX3 — The Electric SUV Pioneer
cards.verdict: BMW's first electric SUV — based on the X3 platform. The iX3 is the accessible entry point to BMW electric SUV ownership. The 320 hp 40 model delivers 395 miles of range and 300 kW charging — the new entry-level EV SUV.

cards.title: i4 — The Electric Gran Coupé
cards.verdict: The electric 4 Series Gran Coupé — BMW's answer to the Tesla Model 3. The eDrive40 delivers 340 miles of range and 286 hp; the M60 xDrive delivers 544 hp and 0-62 mph in 3.9 seconds. The i4 is the sweet spot in the i Series range — proven Gen5 eDrive, sensible values, and genuine driver engagement.

cards.title: i5 — The Electric Executive
cards.verdict: The first electric 5 Series — available as eDrive40 (340 hp, RWD), xDrive40 (394 hp, AWD), and M60 xDrive (601 hp, AWD). The i5 delivers executive luxury with electric performance. The M60 xDrive is the ultimate electric 5 Series — 601 hp and 0-62 mph in 3.8 seconds.

cards.title: i7 — The Electric Flagship 👑 The Ultimate Electric BMW
cards.verdict: The electric 7 Series — BMW's flagship luxury saloon. Available as eDrive50 (455 hp), xDrive60 (544 hp), and the M70 xDrive (680 hp). The i7 M70 delivers 680 hp, 1,100 Nm of torque, and 426 miles of range — the ultimate electric luxury saloon.

cards.title: iX — The Electric SUV Flagship
cards.verdict: BMW's electric SUV flagship — the most technologically advanced BMW ever made. Available as xDrive40 (408 hp), xDrive50 (544 hp), and M60 (659 hp). The iX M60 delivers 659 hp, 1,015 Nm of torque, and 0-62 mph in 3.8 seconds. The iX is the i Series for buyers who want the ultimate electric SUV.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW i Series Electric Motors — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every electric motor and powertrain across the BMW i Series — verified specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: i4, i5, i7 and iX ratings: 85% confidence — electric motors are inherently simpler than combustion engines, but long-term battery degradation data is still emerging. i8 rating: Expert-knowledge tier — the i8's hybrid complexity is well-documented by specialist workshops. Neue Klasse i3 rating: Provisional — the car is too new for volume data; ratings reflect early patterns and BMW's engineering reputation. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The i3 name covers two completely different vehicles — the I01 city car (2013–2022) and the NA0 Neue Klasse saloon (2026–present). These should be treated as separate models. Correction applied: The i8 is a plug-in hybrid, not a full electric vehicle. The 1.5L B38 three-cylinder engine requires traditional engine servicing alongside the electric motor. Correction applied: The Neue Klasse i3 uses sixth-generation BMW eDrive technology with 800V architecture — a significant upgrade from the Gen5 400V systems in the i4, i5 and iX. Correction applied: Battery replacement costs are the defining cost item for i Series ownership — £5,000–£15,000+ depending on model and battery size.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the i Series range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: Early i3 models (22 kWh) suffer from battery degradation — reduced range, increased charging times. Affects i3 60 Ah (2013–2016) and 94 Ah (2017–2018). Battery replacement: £5,000–£10,000+.
Electric motor bearings can wear over time, causing whining noise under load. Affects high-mileage i4, iX and iX3 models. Motor replacement: £3,000–£5,000.
The i8's 7.1–11.6 kWh battery and hybrid system require specialist servicing. Range degradation and charging issues are common as the cars age. Battery replacement: £5,000–£10,000+.
The B38 three-cylinder engine in the i8 can suffer from turbocharger issues, oil leaks, and timing chain stretch — same issues as the combustion-only B38. Affects all i8 models. £2,000–£5,000 repair.
The charging port and onboard charger can fail — preventing AC or DC charging. Affects all i Series models. £500–£2,000 repair.
The iDrive system and battery management software can experience glitches — check for software updates on any used i Series.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW i Series Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the most popular i Series model
⬆ Rising — electric SUV demand is growing
⬆ Rising — the executive EV is gaining traction

insights: Interesting insight: The i Series generates a different kind of enquiry than the rest of the BMW range — owners are asking about battery degradation, charging speeds, and range rather than timing chains or rod bearings. The i4 is the most popular model by some distance — it's the Tesla Model 3 competitor that buyers actually want. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The i3 (I01) is now entering bargain territory — £8,000–£15,000 for a used example. But the battery degradation question means buyers need to budget accordingly: a £5,000–£10,000 battery replacement on a £10,000 car is a serious consideration. The 42 kWh (120 Ah) models are the ones to buy — the larger battery degrades more slowly and delivers better range. (Editorial synthesis.)

The i8 is a future classic — only 20,465 were produced, and the butterfly doors, carbon-fibre chassis, and hybrid powertrain make it unique. But the hybrid complexity means specialist-only servicing — budget for high-voltage battery inspection before buying any used i8. (Editorial synthesis.)

The Neue Klasse i3 (NA0) is the future — 800V architecture, 469 hp, 900 km range, and near-M3 power. Too new for volume reliability data, but the Gen6 eDrive technology represents a significant leap forward. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW i Series Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one i Series based on this page, buy an i4 eDrive40 with the Gen5 eDrive system — it's the sweet spot in the range: 286 hp, 340 miles of range, proven electric motor technology, and sensible used values. If the budget only stretches to an early i3, buy the 42 kWh (120 Ah) model and budget for battery health checks before you buy — a £5,000–£10,000 battery replacement on a £10,000 car is the trap, not the bargain. And on any i8, budget for hybrid system inspection before you buy — it's the most complex BMW you can own.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW i Series Electric Motor & Battery Replacement Costs — By Model
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for electric motor and battery replacement — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting an i Series electric motor typically takes 8–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. High-voltage battery replacement requires specialist training and equipment. The i Series rule — battery is the new engine: A £5,000–£10,000 battery replacement against a £8,000–£15,000 used i3 is 33–125% — ❌ walk away on early 60 Ah models. A £7,000–£11,000 battery against a £20,000–£30,000 i4 is 23–55% — ⚠️ watch. A £9,000–£13,000 battery against a £45,000–£70,000 iX is 13–29% — ✅ safe. Our 60% BMW threshold is the line: below 60% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Buy the newest battery technology you can afford — the 42 kWh i3, Gen5 i4/iX, or Neue Klasse 800V system are the ones to buy.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an i Series electric motor typically takes 8–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. High-voltage battery replacement requires specialist training and equipment. The i Series rule — battery is the new engine: A £5,000–£10,000 battery replacement against a £8,000–£15,000 used i3 is 33–125% — ❌ walk away on early 60 Ah models. A £7,000–£11,000 battery against a £20,000–£30,000 i4 is 23–55% — ⚠️ watch. A £9,000–£13,000 battery against a £45,000–£70,000 iX is 13–29% — ✅ safe. Our 60% BMW threshold is the line: below 60% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Buy the newest battery technology you can afford — the 42 kWh i3, Gen5 i4/iX, or Neue Klasse 800V system are the ones to buy.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW i Series Evolved — 2013 to Today

eras.whyChanged: BMW launched the i3 as the first mass-produced carbon-fibre EV and the i8 as the plug-in hybrid supercar. The i3 established BMW's electric credentials; the i8 defined the brand's hybrid future.
eras.worthKnowing: The i3 is a future classic — the carbon-fibre body and futuristic design make it unique. The i8 is a supercar icon — but the hybrid complexity means specialist-only servicing.

eras.era: The Gen5 Era — i4, iX, iX3
eras.whyChanged: BMW's Gen5 eDrive technology (400V) delivered significant range and charging improvements. The i4 and iX became the mainstream electric models, delivering 340–544 hp and up to 380 miles of range.
eras.worthKnowing: The Gen5 eDrive is proven — the i4 eDrive40 is the sweet spot in the i Series range. The iX is the technology flagship.

eras.keyModels: Neue Klasse i3, iX3 40, i7 M70
eras.whyChanged: BMW's sixth-generation eDrive technology (800V) delivers up to 300–400 kW charging, 550 miles of range, and 10-80% charging in under 20 minutes. The Neue Klasse platform represents BMW's electric future.
eras.worthKnowing: The Neue Klasse i3 is the future — 469 hp, 800V architecture, and near-M3 power. The i7 M70 delivers 680 hp — the most powerful i Series model ever.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW i Series?

profiles.verdict: The i4 eDrive40 is the sweet spot — 286 hp, 340 miles of range, proven Gen5 eDrive, and sensible used values (£20,000–£30,000). The iX is the electric SUV choice.

The i5 and i7 are the electric executive choices — the i5 eDrive40 delivers 340 hp and 373 miles of range; the i7 is the ultimate electric luxury saloon. Buy the newest battery technology you can afford.
The Neue Klasse i3 with 800V architecture and 469 hp is the tech pick — 900 km range, 300–400 kW charging, and near-M3 power. The iX M60 with 659 hp is the flagship.
A cheap early i3 is not a cheap EV — it's an expensive one with the first battery bills prepaid by nobody. A £15,000 i3 120 Ah is the bargain; a £8,000 60 Ah without battery health check is the trap.

The i8 is a future classic — only 20,465 produced, butterfly doors, carbon-fibre chassis, and the B38 three-cylinder engine. The i3 is also a future classic — the first mass-produced carbon-fibre EV. Both are worth proper investment.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW i Series Motor — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Whining noise from an i4 motor? Reduced range on an i3? On the i Series, the answer depends on which component has failed — a motor replacement is routine (£3,000–£5,000); a battery replacement is the defining cost (£5,000–£15,000+). Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
